title: Scholarship Management & Career Roadmap Platform — Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Build the Student Dashboard page with navigation, sidebar, and summary cards
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard renders with sidebar navigation and top navbar.
Summary cards display live data fetched from the backend.
Navigating sidebar links renders the correct section without full page reload.

file_operations.description: Create the main Student Dashboard page with a top navigation bar, sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Scholarships, My Applications, Resume Builder, Documents, Profile), and a content area that displays the active section. Include summary cards showing Profile Completion, Active Applications count, Upcoming Deadlines, and Scholarships Matched. Fetch data from backend methods: getCallerUserProfile, getUserApplications, getProfileCompletion, and listScholarships. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users (#user role) can access this page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable layout component that includes the top navbar, sidebar with navigation links, and a main content slot. This component wraps all authenticated dashboard sections and provides consistent navigation structure across the application.

Create a reusable card component for displaying dashboard summary metrics. Props include title, value (number or string), icon, and optional description. Style with card-based layout using subtle shadows matching the government-portal theme (deep teal and saffron accents).

Add route registration for /dashboard pointing to the Dashboard page component. Ensure the route is protected and requires authentication using the authorization component. Add navigation link in the main app structure if not already present.

requirements.summary: Build a Profile Completion Meter component as a circular SVG progress bar with color-coded thresholds and missing items tooltip
acceptanceCriteria: Circular SVG arc fills proportionally to the completion percentage.
Tooltip appears on hover/tap and lists all missing field labels.
Arc color matches the defined thresholds (red/amber/green).
Component is reusable and accepts percentage and missingItems as props.

file_operations.description: Create a circular SVG progress bar component that displays completion percentage (0-100) as a number inside the circle. The SVG arc color changes based on thresholds: red for 0-40%, amber for 41-79%, green for 80-100%. When completion is below 100%, hovering or tapping shows a tooltip listing each missing item (e.g., 'Upload Marksheet', 'Add Income Certificate'). Props: completionPercentage (number), missingItems (string array). Fetch data using getProfileCompletion backend method when used.

Integrate the ProfileCompletionMeter component into the Dashboard summary cards section. Fetch the profile completion data using getProfileCompletion backend method and pass completionPercentage and missingFields to the meter component.

requirements.summary: Build a Skill Gap Analysis widget showing user skills vs required skills with visual indicators
acceptanceCriteria: Widget fetches the user's skills and the matched scholarship's required skills.
Matched skills display with a green check icon.
Missing skills display with a red alert icon.
Widget updates when the selected scholarship changes.

file_operations.description: Create a widget displaying two columns: 'Your Skills' (from user's career record via getCallerUserProfile) and 'Required Skills' (from the top matched scholarship via getScholarship and checkEligibility). Skills present in both show a green check icon. Skills required but missing show a red alert icon. Props: scholarshipId (optional bigint). When scholarshipId changes, refetch and update the comparison.
Add the SkillGapAnalysis widget to the Dashboard main content area below the summary cards. Pass the top matched scholarshipId (derived from listScholarships filtered by checkEligibility results).

requirements.summary: Build a Scholarship Search & Browse page with search, filters, and card grid
acceptanceCriteria: Search input filters scholarship cards in real time.
Filter controls correctly narrow the displayed results.
Each card displays all required fields.
Eligibility badge reflects the result from the backend eligibility check.

file_operations.description: Create the Scholarship Search & Browse page with a search bar, filter controls (category dropdown, deadline date range picker, provider text input), and a grid of scholarship cards. Each card displays: title, provider, deadline, short description, 'Apply Now' button, and an eligibility badge ('Eligible' in green or 'Check Eligibility' in amber). Fetch scholarships using listScholarships and check eligibility for each using checkEligibility. Search filters cards client-side by keyword. Clicking 'Apply Now' navigates to /scholarships/:id. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users can access this page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable card component for displaying a single scholarship. Props: scholarship (Scholarship type from backend.d.ts), isEligible (boolean), onApplyClick (callback). Display title, provider, deadline, description, eligibility badge, and 'Apply Now' button. Style with card-based layout using subtle shadows and the deep teal/saffron theme.

Add route registration for /scholarships pointing to the Scholarships page component. Ensure the route is protected and requires authentication. Add a navigation link to 'Scholarships' in the sidebar (via DashboardLayout or main navigation).

requirements.summary: Build a Scholarship Application detail page with eligibility summary, document checklist, and pre-filled form
acceptanceCriteria: 'Auto-Fill from Profile' button populates the form with data returned from the backend.
Eligibility summary section is visible above the form.
Submit button is disabled and shows a tooltip when required documents are missing.
On successful submission, the application status is set to 'Submitted' and a success message is shown.

file_operations.description: Create the Scholarship Application detail page that displays scholarship info, eligibility summary (eligible/not eligible + missing requirements list from checkEligibility), document checklist (see REQ-12 component), and a pre-filled application form. Include an 'Auto-Fill from Profile' button that calls autoFillApplication backend method and populates all form fields. The form can only be submitted when all required documents are uploaded (check via getCallerUserProfile documents array). On submit, call applyToScholarship with status 'submitted'. Show a success toast on completion. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users can access this page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add route registration for /scholarships/:id pointing to the ScholarshipDetail page component. Ensure the route is protected and requires authentication.

requirements.summary: Build a Document Checklist UI component showing uploaded vs missing documents with upload functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Uploaded documents show a green check and their filename.
Missing documents show a red alert icon and an 'Upload' button.
Uploading a file from the checklist updates the document status immediately.
Component accepts a documents list prop and is reusable across pages.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable component that renders a list of required documents. Props: requiredDocuments (string array from scholarship), userDocuments (DocumentReference array from getCallerUserProfile). Each row shows the document name; if uploaded (found in userDocuments with uploadStatus true), show a green checkmark icon and the filename. If not uploaded, show a red alert icon labeled 'Missing' and an 'Upload' button that triggers a file picker. On file selection, update the user's MasterUserRecord documents array via saveCallerUserProfile (add a new DocumentReference with fileName, documentType, uploadStatus=true). Component is reusable on ScholarshipDetail page and standalone Documents section.

Create a standalone Documents page that displays all documents uploaded by the user. Use the DocumentChecklist component with all document types (fetch from getCallerUserProfile documents array). Allow users to upload new documents or replace existing ones. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users can access this page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add route registration for /documents pointing to the Documents page component. Ensure the route is protected and requires authentication. Add a navigation link to 'Documents' in the sidebar.

requirements.summary: Build a Resume Builder page with tabbed Academic and Career forms and auto-fill functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Both Academic and Career tabs render correct form fields.
'Auto-Fill from Profile' populates fields from the user's stored record.
'Save to Profile' calls the backend update function and shows a success toast.
Form validation highlights empty required fields before saving.

file_operations.description: Create the Resume Builder page with two tabbed sections: 'Academic Details' and 'Career Details'. Academic Details form collects: institution, degree, field of study (not in backend type but infer from degree), start/end year, percentage/CGPA, marksheet reference. Career Details form collects: employer, job title (role), start/end date (duration), description (not in backend type, omit or use role), skills (comma-separated array). Each tab includes an 'Auto-Fill from Profile' button that fetches getCallerUserProfile and populates form fields from academics and career arrays. A 'Save to Profile' button calls saveCallerUserProfile with the updated MasterUserRecord. Show form validation errors and a success toast on save. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users can access this page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add route registration for /resume-builder pointing to the ResumeBuilder page component. Ensure the route is protected and requires authentication. Add a navigation link to 'Resume Builder' in the sidebar.

requirements.summary: Add a multilingual toggle button switching UI between English and Hindi with full translation support
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar toggle button switches language between English and Hindi.
All visible text on the dashboard, forms, checklists, and navigation updates instantly on toggle.
Selected language is persisted in localStorage and restored on page reload.
No untranslated English strings remain visible when Hindi is active.

file_operations.description: Create an i18n translation module exporting a translations object with keys for 'en' (English) and 'hi' (Hindi). Include translation keys for all user-facing labels, headings, button text, tooltips, placeholder text, and navigation items across Dashboard, Scholarships, My Applications, Resume Builder, Documents, Profile, eligibility messages, status indicators, form labels, and error messages.

Create a custom React hook useTranslation that manages the current language state (default 'en'), reads/writes to localStorage key 'appLanguage', and provides a t(key: string) function to retrieve translated strings from the translations module. Also provide a toggleLanguage() function that switches between 'en' and 'hi'.

Create a language toggle button component that uses useTranslation hook to switch between English and Hindi. Display current language label (e.g., 'EN' / 'HI') and toggle on click. Style as a compact button suitable for placement in the top navbar.
Integrate the LanguageToggle component into the top navigation bar. Replace all hardcoded text labels in the navbar and sidebar navigation with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded text labels, headings, tooltips, and button text with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded text labels, search placeholder, filter labels, and button text with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded text labels, form labels, button text, and eligibility messages with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded tab labels, form field labels, button text, and validation messages with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded text labels, document type names, and button text with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace all hardcoded status labels ('Missing', 'Uploaded'), button text, and tooltips with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace tooltip text and missing item labels with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.
Replace column headers ('Your Skills', 'Required Skills') and status labels with translated strings using the useTranslation hook.

requirements.summary: Build an FAQ/Help Chatbot widget with keyword-based matching supporting English and Hinglish queries
acceptanceCriteria: Floating chat button is visible on all pages.
Chat window opens/closes on button click.
Bot matches user input to the knowledge base and returns an answer with a page link.
Keyword matching handles both English and common Hindi/Hinglish variants of document and feature names.
If no match is found, the bot returns a helpful fallback message with a link to the Help section.

file_operations.description: Create a JSON knowledge base containing an array of FAQ entries. Each entry has: id, patterns (array of keyword strings including English and Hindi/Hinglish variants like 'marksheet', 'marksheet kahan', 'upload certificate', etc.), answer (string), and pageLink (internal route like /documents, /scholarships, /resume-builder). Include entries for common queries: where to upload documents, how to apply, how to fill profile, eligibility check, skill gap, etc.

Create a keyword matching utility function that takes user input (string) and the knowledge base (array), normalizes both (lowercase, trim, remove diacritics), and finds the best matching entry by checking if any pattern keywords appear in the input. Return the matched entry or null. Support both English and Hinglish (transliterated Hindi) keyword matching.

Create a floating chatbot widget with a button fixed at the bottom-right corner of the screen. When clicked, open a chat window overlay with a message input field and a message history area. On user message submit, use chatbotMatcher to find a matching FAQ entry, display the answer text, and render a clickable link to the pageLink route. If no match, show a fallback message: 'I couldn't find an answer. Please visit our Help section.' with a link to /help. Support translated UI labels using useTranslation hook.
Integrate the ChatbotWidget component at the root level so it appears on all pages. Place it outside the main routing area so it persists across route changes.

Create a Help page that displays all FAQ entries from the chatbot knowledge base in a structured format (accordion or expandable list). Each entry shows the question/topic and the full answer. Include navigation back to the dashboard.
Add route registration for /help pointing to the Help page component. This route does not require authentication (accessible to guests for public FAQ browsing).

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive visual theme using deep teal and saffron colors, government-portal styling, and consistent design system
acceptanceCriteria: A consistent color palette (deep teal primary, saffron accent) is applied platform-wide via Tailwind config.
Typography is uniform across all headings, body text, and labels.
Card components have consistent border-radius, shadow, and padding.
Color-coded status indicators (green/amber/red) are used consistently for eligibility, documents, and profile completion.

file_operations.description: Extend the Tailwind theme with a custom color palette: primary deep teal (e.g., #006666 or #004d4d), saffron accent (e.g., #FF9933), success green, warning amber, and error red. Define consistent spacing, border-radius (e.g., rounded-lg for cards), and shadow utilities (e.g., shadow-md for cards). Set default sans-serif font family for professional typography.

Define global CSS rules for consistent typography (headings, body text, labels) using the Tailwind theme. Apply base styles for institutional feel: professional sans-serif font, clean line-height, and accessible contrast ratios. Define reusable utility classes for status indicators: .status-eligible (green), .status-pending (amber), .status-rejected (red), and .status-missing (red alert).

Apply consistent card styling: border-radius (rounded-lg), subtle shadow (shadow-md), padding (p-6), and background (bg-white). Use the deep teal and saffron color scheme for icon accents and borders.
Apply consistent card styling matching SummaryCard. Use status-eligible and status-pending classes for the eligibility badge. Ensure 'Apply Now' button uses saffron accent color.

Apply the deep teal primary color to the top navbar and sidebar background. Use white or light gray text for contrast. Apply structured grid layout for the main content area. Ensure navigation links have hover states using saffron accent.
Use the defined status colors for the circular arc: red for 0-40%, amber for 41-79%, green for 80-100%. Ensure consistent styling with the overall theme.

Use status-eligible class for green checkmarks on uploaded documents and status-missing class for red alert icons on missing documents. Ensure button styling matches the theme (saffron for primary actions).
Use status-eligible class for green check icons on matched skills and status-missing class for red alert icons on missing skills. Apply card-based layout with consistent styling.

Wire the generated institutional hero banner image (abstract geometric pattern in deep teal and saffron tones) into the Dashboard page as a header background image using the workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/dashboard-hero-banner.dim_1200x300.png. Display it at the top of the Dashboard content area.

Wire the generated circular scholarship portal logo (graduation cap and upward arrow motif in teal and saffron) into the top navbar using the workspace file path frontend/public/assets/generated/scholarpath-logo.dim_128x128.png. Display it as the app logo in the DashboardLayout navbar.
Reference the generated logo image frontend/public/assets/generated/scholarpath-logo.dim_128x128.png in the navbar. Display it as an element with appropriate alt text ('ScholarPath Portal Logo').

Reference the generated hero banner image frontend/public/assets/generated/dashboard-hero-banner.dim_1200x300.png as a background image or element at the top of the main content area. Ensure it spans the full width and maintains aspect ratio.
